purpose.summary: The CBPAUP0C program deletes authorization details and summaries from a database and takes checkpoints during the process. It deletes authorization details and summaries based on application IDs and handles checkpointing to ensure data consistency.

called_programs.purpose: Abends the program when a DLI operation fails.
Abends the program when a DLI operation fails.
Abends the program when a checkpoint fails.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph deletes authorization detail records from the database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the PA-ACCT-ID if it is. It then executes a DLI DELETE operation on the PAUTDTL1 segment using data from PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. If the delete is successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-DTL-DELETED. If the delete fails, it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The purpose of this paragraph is to remove specific authorization details that are no longer needed. It consumes PA-ACCT-ID and PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS as inputs and updates WS-NO-DTL-DELETED or triggers program termination as outputs. The paragraph handles error conditions by checking DIBSTAT and abending if the delete fails.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.

This paragraph deletes authorization summary records from the database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the PA-ACCT-ID if it is. It then executes a DLI DELETE operation on the PAUTSUM0 segment using data from PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY. If the delete is successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED. If the delete fails, it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The purpose of this paragraph is to remove authorization summaries that are no longer needed. It consumes PA-ACCT-ID and PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY as inputs and updates WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED or triggers program termination as outputs. The paragraph handles error conditions by checking DIBSTAT and abending if the delete fails.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.

This paragraph takes a checkpoint using the DLI CHKP command. It uses WK-CHKPT-ID as the checkpoint identifier. If the checkpoint is successful (DIBSTAT is spaces), it increments WS-NO-CHKP. If WS-NO-CHKP reaches P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, it resets WS-NO-CHKP to 0 and displays a checkpoint success message including WS-NO-SUMRY-READ and WS-CURR-APP-ID. If the checkpoint fails, it displays an error message with DIBSTAT, WS-NO-SUMRY-READ, and WS-CURR-APP-ID and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The purpose of this paragraph is to periodically save the program's state to allow for restart in case of failure. It consumes WK-CHKPT-ID, P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, WS-NO-SUMRY-READ, and WS-CURR-APP-ID as inputs and updates WS-NO-CHKP or triggers program termination as outputs. The paragraph handles error conditions by checking DIBSTAT and abending if the checkpoint fails.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.

This paragraph is the abend routine for the program. It displays a message indicating that the program is abending, sets the RETURN-CODE to 16, and then terminates the program using GOBACK. It does not consume any inputs, but sets the RETURN-CODE as an output. The purpose of this paragraph is to provide a consistent way to terminate the program when an unrecoverable error occurs.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It is a standard COBOL construct for exiting a paragraph.
